evidence.scientific_clarity: Violin+box+jitter design clearly shows dose-dependent viability reduction; Control > Drug-A 10uM > Drug-A 50uM > Combo with significance brackets; brief's scientific claim is fully carried.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 is NOT set; PDF embeds DejaVuSans as Type3 (bitmap glyphs, not selectable/editable vector text); DejaVu Sans is the matplotlib default which should be replaced.
evidence.palette: Four-color scheme (#4C9BE8, #F4A460, #E86060, #7B68EE) is reasonably CB-safe; red (#E86060) is used for Drug-A 50uM (directional intensity) which is acceptable, but red is not reserved exclusively for a directional role — minor concern.
evidence.layout_density: Panel space is well-used; significance brackets and points fit within ylim without crowding; no gaping whitespace; labels do not overlap.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: No legend is present (colors not explained in figure; relies entirely on x-axis labels which do carry the information); caption matches figure content well; bracket logic is correct.
evidence.vector_fidelity: figure.pdf is produced but uses Type3 (bitmap) fonts because pdf.fonttype=42 was never set; text is NOT selectable in the PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png, figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all exist and are non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption states n=100 per group, explains violin/box/jitter encodings, defines significance notation, and makes a specific directional claim (dose-dependent suppression, Combo greatest).

overall_strengths: Well-constructed violin+box+jitter design that clearly conveys the brief's scientific message with significance brackets.
Caption is thorough: states sample size, explains all visual encodings, defines p-value thresholds, and ends with a specific finding.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 (and rcParams['svg.fonttype']='none') is missing, so the PDF embeds DejaVuSans as Type3 bitmap glyphs — text is not selectable or editable in the vector output.
Default DejaVu Sans font is used throughout; a publication-quality figure should set a more appropriate typeface (e.g., Helvetica, Arial, or a serif like Times) via rcParams.
